title: SuperBEST v4 — Portfolio Theory & Risk Metrics: Exact Node Costs

equations.name: Sharpe Ratio

breakdown.note: excess return — signed; R_p - R_f can be negative

assumptions: σ_p is precomputed (e.g., from portfolio variance formula fin2_03)
Result can be negative (bad Sharpe); signed output

equations.name: CAPM Expected Return

breakdown.note: R_f > 0, beta_term > 0 for positive β — add_pos applies

assumptions: If β < 0 (inverse/hedge asset): beta_term < 0, must use add_gen → cost becomes 15n

equations.name: Portfolio Variance (2-asset)

breakdown.note: both are squares — provably ≥ 0; add_pos valid
cross term ∈ [-1, 1] range when ρ < 0 — signed; must use add_gen

notes: If ρ ≥ 0 is guaranteed by domain assumption: replace add_gen with add_pos → saves 8n, total = 34n
Extends linearly to N-asset case: O(N²) cross terms dominate

equations.name: Value at Risk (Normal Approximation)

breakdown.note: z_α is a precomputed constant (e.g., 1.645 for 95% CI)

assumptions: z_α treated as a precomputed constant — erfinv (normal quantile) is outside the EML closure
Result is a loss threshold; signed output expected
equations.eml_boundary_note: Normal inverse CDF (erfinv) cannot be computed in EML without approximation. z_α must be injected as a constant or approximated via polynomial — not a native EML primitive.

equations.name: Kelly Criterion (Simplified Continuous)

breakdown.note: optimal fraction; signed if μ < 0 (short position)

assumptions: Simplified log-utility Kelly; full formula includes μ - r_f in numerator

equations.name: Bond Price (Coupon Bond)

steps.note: discount factor base; t is integer index

coupon_sum_accumulation.note: All PV(coupon) terms are positive; (N-1) add_pos operations to sum them

face_value_term.note: add_pos(1,r) shared with coupon computation — cost 3n paid once

equations.cross_domain: Coupon term structure (add_pos → pow → recip → mul = 9n) is isomorphic to population growth discounting term (N_0 · e^{-λt} motif) in pharmacokinetics/epidemiology. Same DAG pattern, different domain labels.

equations.name: Modified Duration

steps.note: time-weighted PV; t is a scalar constant per term

equations.note: Assumes bond price P is precomputed from fin2_06 (otherwise add 12N+6 to total)

equations.name: Log Return

breakdown.note: price ratio; positive by domain assumption (prices > 0)
ln is a native EML primitive — 1n

equations.note: ln is deeply EML-native. Log returns are the most cost-efficient return measure in the EML framework.
equations.cross_domain: Identical DAG to pharmacokinetic half-life computation: ln(C_t / C_0) where C is drug concentration. 3n motif appears across biology, physics (radioactive decay), and finance.

equations.name: CAGR (Compound Annual Growth Rate)

breakdown.note: subtract 1 to get net growth rate

equations.cross_domain: CAGR = geometric mean of growth factors. Same pow(ratio, recip_n) motif appears in fractal dimension estimation (box-counting: D = ln(N)/ln(1/r) rewritten as ratio^(1/n)) and in geometric mean of biological fitness metrics.

equations.name: Sortino Ratio
equations.sigma_d_computation_note: σ_d (downside deviation) requires: for each return r_t, compute min(r_t, 0)² and average. The min() operation is a conditional selection — not a pure EML arithmetic primitive. This requires either: (a) a ReLU-like approximation via smooth max, or (b) external preprocessing to mask negative returns before entering the EML graph. σ_d is NOT computable in closed-form EML without approximation or preprocessing.

summary_table.name: Value at Risk (Normal)
summary_table.cost_note: z_α precomputed constant; erfinv outside EML

summary_table.name: Sortino Ratio
summary_table.cost_note: given σ_d; σ_d itself requires conditional logic outside EML

instances: information theory: entropy H = -Σ p·ln(p) (per term: 3n + neg)

cross_domain_connections.motif: add_pos → pow → recip → mul — 9n discount term

instances: fractal geometry: box-counting dimension D = log(N)/log(1/r)
biology: geometric mean fitness across generations

eml_boundary_cases.name: Normal Quantile (erfinv)
eml_boundary_cases.verdict: Outside EML closure. Must inject z_α as a precomputed constant.
eml_boundary_cases.workaround: Polynomial approximation of erfinv (Abramowitz & Stegun) — adds ~10-20n per evaluation with bounded error

eml_boundary_cases.name: Conditional selection / min(r, 0)
eml_boundary_cases.verdict: min() is not a pure EML arithmetic node. Requires smooth approximation (softmin) or external preprocessing.
eml_boundary_cases.workaround: Smooth approximation: min(x,0) ≈ (x - |x|)/2; |x| = sqrt(x²) = sqrt(pow(x,2)) → 3+2+2 = 7n per return, then square (3n) → 10n per return
